weather_report:
Late morning in West Perth arrives with a gentle light and a veil of high cloud, softening edges and muting the glare. The sun feels courteous rather than insistent; the UV index will peak at a modest-to-moderate level around midday, then ease as the cloud thickens. Air clarity is good, the sky a cool wash rather than a hard blue, the kind of day that invites a quieter pace.

By mid‑afternoon an overcast lid is likely to settle in, then break open to scattered clouds toward late afternoon, offering a few clean, silvery moments before dusk. It looks pleasantly subdued rather than dreary. Dress in light layers—long sleeves or a soft jacket—and keep sunglasses handy for those brief, brighter breaks. Good weather for unhurried errands on foot, a small loop through Kings Park, leafing through a bookshop without a rush, or tidying the balcony plants. If you’re lingering outdoors toward late afternoon, the softened sun will make a short spin on the bike or a stroll to the corner cafe feel just right.
